meaning: n.[植]罗望子,罗望子的果实
examples: They will eat mostly rice and sauces made from yogurt and tamarind, spiced with peppers.
You can use lime juice if you don't have any tamarind.
You can use lime juice if you don't have any tamarind .
More unusual ingredients, such as tamarind paste and aromatic curry leaves, can be found in Indian grocery stores or specialty shops.
The menu may include such flavours as tamarind, guava, walnut and strawberry, and from time to time, beet ("for the heart") and celery ("for diabetes") make an appearance.
